title: Market Brief — Tuesday, April 21, 2026
summary: Compute is in severe shortage across all GPU classes. H100 availability sits at 8% (112/339), H200 at 18% (67/351), and B200 at 17% (118/417), all well below the 25% scarcity threshold. The headline move is B200 spot surging 20.6% day-over-day to $4.75/hr as utilization rocketed 39.6pp in 30 days to 83.4%, signaling acute demand from next-gen inference workloads bidding up the newest silicon.
paragraphs: Compute is in severe shortage across all GPU classes. H100 availability sits at 8% (112/339), H200 at 18% (67/351), and B200 at 17% (118/417), all well below the 25% scarcity threshold. The headline move is B200 spot surging 20.6% day-over-day to $4.75/hr as utilization rocketed 39.6pp in 30 days to 83.4%, signaling acute demand from next-gen inference workloads bidding up the newest silicon.

At TCO-adjusted costs (1.25x spot), H100 inference runs $6.83/M tokens and H200 runs $8.35/M tokens on Llama 70B. DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.378/M output is burning cash at massive scale, selling roughly $3.00 below breakeven on efficient hardware. OpenAI's mid-tier GPT-5 at $10/M output and Google's Gemini 2.5 Pro at $10/M hold positive margins on B200 (TCO $4.34/M). Grades: OpenAI Strong, Anthropic Neutral, Google Neutral, DeepSeek Burning Cash.

B200 spot at $4.75/hr with 85.9% annualized volatility is overvalued short-term given thin liquidity and momentum-driven pricing. A100s at $1.07/hr OCPI with utilization dropping 12pp to 78% are undervalued for cost-optimized batch inference workloads, representing the clearest relative-value trade in the GPU complex today.
bullets: 8% of H100 GPUs are available, the tightest supply across all three compute classes.
118 of 417 B200 GPUs are free, but utilization surged 39.6 percentage points in 30 days.
$4.75/hr B200 spot is up 20.6% in a single day, the largest move in the stack.
$0.378/M output makes DeepSeek V3.2 roughly $3.00/M below TCO-adjusted breakeven on B200.
21.41 on the LLMflation Index means token prices have fallen 79% since GPT-4 launched at base 100.
$975 per DDR5 32GB RDIMM is up 4.84% week-over-week, pressuring inference cost floors.
